sglang.title: kernel: use tensor cores for flashinfer gqa kernels
sglang.author: yzh119
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This pr fixes the kernel dispatch rule for flashinfer backend.
Motivation
When group size > 4, using tensor cores is faster.

Hello, I am willhe from the XVERSE Infra Team.

sglang.title: [Feature] Initial support for multi-LoRA serving
sglang.author: Ying1123
sglang.bodyExcerpt: This PR gives initial multi-LoRA serving support. Currently, it supports LoRA on attention (qkvo) and mlp (gate, up, down) linear layers. It supports dynamic loading and offloading, but it does not support unified memory. The memory pool for LoRA adapters is pre-allocated. Please use a smaller --mem

vllm.title: [torch.compile] hide slicing under custom op for inductor
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
see pytorch/pytorch#131192
when inductor sees a view being mutated, it will copy the tensor.
hiding the slicing operation under custom op solves the issue for inductor.

vllm.title: [Hotfix][VLM] Fixing max position embeddings for Pixtral
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, max position embeddings was missing from the config and thus set to 8192 by default, causing generation issue when current context window is over 8192.
This PR hotfixes this issue.
cc @patrickvonplaten @simon-mo
Co-authored-by: Woosuk Kwon woosuk.kwon@berkeley.edu

vllm.title: [BugFix] lazy init _copy_stream to avoid torch init wrong gpu instance
vllm.author: lnykww
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Because MultiStepModelRunner is initialized before init_device, torch.cuda.Stream() will use the wrong GPU instance in multi GPU environment. lazy init _copy_stream to fix this issue.

vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes a bug that I believe was introduced by the mistral model format support in #8168
The transformers_utils/config.py file now uses an unsafe file_exists check that will immediately fail in offline mode. This takes down all of our deployments where we set HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 to ensure that we

vllm.title: [Core] Add engine option to return only deltas or final output
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The LLMEngine and AsyncLLMEngine APIs will currently return/stream cumulative outputs and prompt-related data for all sequences at every step.
This is more data than needed for LLM.generate or the OpenAI server APIs:

For LLM.generate and non-streaming APIs we only need the final output
For streamin

vllm.title: [Hotfix][Core][VLM] Disable chunked prefill by default and prefix caching for multimodal models
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, multimodal models do not work quite well with chunked prefill and prefix caching

For chunked prefill, this is somewhat working but very flaky: we will run into errors when the available prefill slots in the batch are not enough for the multimodal placeholders for one multimodal item, sin

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Disable multi-node test for InternVL2
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Multi-node test for InternVL2 has been broken on main branch and blocking a lot of PRs. Multinode support for VLMs in particular isn't of high priority of the vLLM project now, so we disable this test for InternVL for now and fix it later.
cc @Isotr0py @Manikandan-Thangaraj-ZS0321 if you have any in

vllm.author: patrickvonplaten
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FILL IN THE PR DESCRIPTION HERE

FIX #8411
This PR fixes a couple bugs that arrive from using chunked pre-filling and previously incorrect image processing.

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There is a function called wait_for_gpu_memory_to_clear in test/utils.py that checks the GPU mem state to prepare the hardware for multiple e2e tests.
Currently, this function is implemented based on nvml, which obtains GPU usage info by physical device number. Therefore, it is not aligned with the

vllm.author: DarkLight1337

I can verify that the Pixtral tests after #8415 pass on my L40. However having pickle files in the repo may be flagged as a security risk, so I'm changing them to JSON in this PR.
cc @patrickvonplaten

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix async log stats
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the issue described here: #8219 (comment)
The fix is simple, we need to skip log stats for sequences that are already preempted since their state is reset to PREFILL (for recompute).

vllm.title: [bugfix] torch profiler bug for single gpu with GPUExecutor
vllm.author: SolitaryThinker
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
GPUExecutor has a different API and does not define a _run_workers.
Another way to fix this would be define the _run_workers (it would only call the driver_worker) api in GPUExecutor to match the API of the other executors. That will avoid the extra import..
closes #8326 and closes #8351
